title: Check if a string is a substring of another

content.problemStatement:
Given two strings, `S1` and `S2`, your task is to determine whether `S2` is a substring of `S1`. If `S2` is a substring of `S1`, print "YES". Otherwise, print "NO".

A substring is a contiguous sequence of characters within a string. For example, "abc" is a substring of "aabcda", but "ac" is not a contiguous sequence in "aabcda".


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer `T`, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains the string `S1`.
The second line contains the string `S2`.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing either "YES" or "NO", depending on whether `S2` is a substring of `S1`.
constraints: S1 and S2 consist only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains the string S1, the second line contains the string S2. Both strings consist only of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing "YES" if S2 is a substring of S1, otherwise print "NO".

sampleTestCases.input:
4
hello
ell
codechef
chef
programming
debug
abcd
efgh
sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first case "ell" appears in "hello". In the second case "chef" appears in "codechef". The third and fourth cases have no occurrence, so the answer is NO.
The whole string S2 equals S1, therefore it is a substring.
S2 is longer than S1, so it cannot be a substring.

solution.approach: The problem asks whether one string appears contiguously inside another. The standard library provides an O(|S1|+|S2|) search (e.g., KMP) but for the given limits a direct call to the built‑in contains/find method is sufficient. For each test case we read S1 and S2 and check S1.contains(S2). This runs in linear time in the length of S1 for each test case, which easily satisfies the constraints.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S1|) per test case, O(total length of all S1) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input strings.

code.Java: import java.io.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-- > 0){String S1=br.readLine().trim();String S2=br.readLine().trim();sb.append(S1.contains(S2)?"YES":"NO").append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
